title: rubus aculeo-idaeus
intro: Rubus aculeo-idaeus, also known as the thorny bramble or raspberry-like thorned raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, derived from its leaves, roots, and stems. The health benefits of Rubus aculeo-idaeus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against various diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Rubus aculeo-idaeus is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
